text: MARKETS: Brent $110.82 (+38.9%); WTI $112.00 (+48.3%). OPEC+ agrees 206k bpd increase for May (Saudi+Russia +62k each). Gas $4.10/gal. FAO Food Index 128.5 (+2.4% MoM). Hormuz Day 35 — effectively closed. Trump deadline 8PM ET Tuesday.
verificationNote: Investing.com, Enerdata, AAA, FAO confirmed.

text: Day 38 AM summary (11:34 PM PDT): Haifa 2 dead/2 missing; cluster bombs Petah Tikva+Haifa; Lebanon 11 killed; Hezbollah warship claim (denied); IRGC 'new Gulf order'; Iran rejects Trump deadline; Masnaa crossing closed; OPEC+ +206k bpd; Brent $110.82/WTI $112.00; gas $4.10; April 7 deadline TOMORROW.
verificationNote: Compiled from JPost, Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, MTV Lebanon, Arab News, NBC News, Enerdata.

text: BREAKING: Israel confirms targeted killing of IRGC Intelligence Chief Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi + Quds Force Unit 840 commander Asghar Bagheri in dawn Tehran strike. IRGC's effective No. 2 eliminated. Biggest decapitation strike since Soleimani 2020.
verificationNote: AP, Middle East Eye, NBC News, Xinhua confirmed.

text: South Pars petrochemical complex at Asaluyeh confirmed destroyed by Israeli strikes — 5 killed, 170 injured. Israeli Defense Ministry: 85% of Iran's petrochemical exports now offline. Combined with previous Mahshahr strikes, Iran's non-oil revenue severely crippled.
verificationNote: PressTV, Middle East Eye, The Logical Indian confirmed.

text: Iranian cluster munitions impact assessment: 28+ bomblet sites across Ramat Gan, Bnei Brak, Givatayim, Petah Tikva. 2 killed (elderly couple 70s), 1 moderate wound. Heavy building damage. First confirmed cluster warhead use on densely populated Tel Aviv suburbs. Escalatory.
verificationNote: Haaretz, Times of Israel, Israel National News confirmed.

text: ISLAMABAD ACCORD: Egypt/Pakistan/Turkey submit 45-day ceasefire proposal. Phase 1: immediate ceasefire + Hormuz reopening. Phase 2: permanent deal in 15-20 days. Pakistan FM Munir in overnight talks with Vance + Araghchi. Iran: will NOT reopen Hormuz under temporary deal.
verificationNote: Axios, CBC, CNBC, Al Jazeera, Al-Monitor confirmed.

text: Qatar LNG tankers Al Daayen and Rasheeda attempting first Hormuz exit since Feb 28. Al Daayen at 26.10N/55.77E moving 8.8kn toward China (149,613 cbm LNG). Rasheeda pulled back. Iraqi tanker 'Ocean Thunder' (~1M bbl Basrah Heavy) already transited under new Iraq exemption.
verificationNote: Bloomberg confirmed both Qatar LNG transit attempt and Iraqi tanker transit.

text: Day 38 PM summary (05:18 AM PDT): Khademi+Bagheri killed Tehran; South Pars destroyed (5 dead, 170 hurt); Sharif Univ struck; cluster bombs kill 2 Ramat Gan (28+ sites); Haifa 4 dead confirmed; 45-day ceasefire proposal; Qatar LNG Hormuz; Iraqi exemption; IRGC claims USS Tripoli (unverified); Brent $110.82/WTI $111.54; gas $4.104; April 7 deadline TOMORROW.
verificationNote: Compiled from AP, Haaretz, Axios, Bloomberg, Times of Israel, NBC News, AAA.

text: BREAKING: Planet Labs has imposed indefinite commercial satellite imagery blackout on Iran at US government request, retroactive to March 9. Replaces 14-day delay with "managed access" model. Independent damage assessment now impossible.
verificationNote: Confirmed by SatNews reporting.

text: Iran rehabilitating missile sites within hours of US/Israeli strikes per US intelligence. IRGC showing remarkable damage-repair speed despite sustained bombing campaign now in 6th week.
verificationNote: Based on Daily News Egypt report citing US intelligence sources.

text: IRGC Intelligence Chief Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi confirmed killed overnight Apr 5-6 alongside Quds Force Unit 840 commander Asghar Bagheri. Khademi described as effectively No. 2 within IRGC. This is 40th+ senior Iranian official eliminated.
verificationNote: IDF confirmed via Fox News, Jerusalem Post.

text: Trump press conference: "The entire country can be taken out in one night, and that night might be tomorrow night." Sets final Hormuz deadline Tuesday 8pm ET. Power Plant Day + Bridge Day threatened. This is the most direct threat of total destruction yet.
verificationNote: Directly quoted from White House press conference. Multiple outlets confirmed.

text: OPEC+ confirms 206,000 bpd output hike for May — largely symbolic as only 2% of Hormuz-disrupted supply. Oil can't transit Hormuz anyway. Brent down slightly to $109.90, WTI $111.81. Markets bracing for Trump Tuesday deadline.
verificationNote: OPEC+ confirmed via Euronews, Al Jazeera, CNBC.

text: Araghchi on CBS: "We don't see any reason why we should talk with Americans." Also confirmed Lavrov phone call. Iran's position hardening ahead of Trump's Tuesday deadline — diplomatic window narrowing rapidly.
verificationNote: CBS Face the Nation broadcast confirmed.

text: Pope Leo XIV Easter message: "Let those who have weapons lay them down!" Hoped Trump would find "off-ramp." First pope to directly reference a sitting US president in Easter address in modern history. Vatican reportedly in back-channel contact with Tehran.
verificationNote: Confirmed via Washington Post, NBC, Axios.

text: Zelensky reveals Russia provided Iran with satellite intelligence on 50-53 Israeli energy grid targets in three strategic tiers. Orot Rabin named as primary target. Russia called Israel an 'energy island.' Massive intelligence escalation.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Kyiv Post and Jerusalem Post.

text: CRITICAL: Iran adviser Velayati threatens to close Bab al-Mandeb alongside Hormuz. 'Resistance front views Bab al-Mandeb as it does Hormuz.' If both straits closed simultaneously, ~25% of global energy blocked. JPMorgan: oil could hit $120-$150.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera reporting.

text: Day 38 daily report: 455 Iranian attack waves against Israel since war began. 18 attack waves toward northern Israel past week (March 30-April 5). Daily average down to 7.7/day. 1,121 Hezbollah attack waves since March 2.
verificationNote: Published on Alma Research Center website.

text: VP Vance reportedly in overnight talks with Pakistan FM Munir and Iranian FM Araghchi via Islamabad channel. Two-phase ceasefire deal on table. Reports say a deal 'could be today.' Deadline 8PM ET Tuesday.
verificationNote: Reported by Breitbart, CNBC.

text: Oil surging ahead of Trump deadline: Brent $111.25 (+39.3%), WTI $110.13 (+45.8%). Physical dated Brent hit $141 on Apr 2 — highest since 2008. IEA warns 'April will be much worse than March.' IEA 400M barrel release barely denting prices.
verificationNote: Oil prices confirmed via Fortune, Investing.com.

text: Pentagon confirms 15,000+ enemy targets hit in Iran conflict. 13 US KIA, 365 WIA. Hegseth compared F-15 pilot rescue to Easter resurrection. Iran Health Ministry: 2,076+ killed. HRANA independent count: 3,540 killed (1,616 civilians, 244+ children).
verificationNote: Pentagon figures from PBS/Military.com. Iran figures from Al Jazeera tracker.

text: DEADLINE DAY: DefSec Hegseth confirms Tuesday will see the most intense day of US strikes on Iran since the war began on Feb 28. Monday was already the highest-volume day. 15,000+ targets hit total. Trump 8pm ET deadline expires TONIGHT.
verificationNote: PBS, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel confirmed Hegseth statements.

text: Iran formally rejects 45-day ceasefire proposal from Egypt/Pakistan/Turkey mediators. Cairo mission: "We only accept end of war with guarantees." Pakistan mediation channel collapsed April 3. Saudi quadrilateral initiative also rejected. No active diplomatic channel remains.
verificationNote: NPR, Bloomberg, RFE/RL, PBS confirmed ceasefire rejection.

text: Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly in 30 days since March 8 election. No photo, video, or audio. CIA actively searching for his location. Israeli official: "No evidence he is actually giving orders." IRGC believed to be making wartime decisions. Pezeshkian unable to get meeting.
verificationNote: JNS, Axios, Al Jazeera, The Week India reporting. Health/location unconfirmed.

text: Saudi air defenses intercepted 7 Iranian ballistic missiles + 10 attack drones over Riyadh (April 5). Second deliberate strike on Saudi capital — timed to diplomatic gathering. Gulf states on maximum alert ahead of Trump deadline tonight.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, House of Saud reporting confirmed.

text: Lebanon front update: 1,461 killed, 4,430 wounded since Feb 28. Hezbollah southern front commander Haj Youssef Ismail Hashem killed by Israeli naval strike. Israel deepens southern Lebanon offensive, destroying entire villages. UN warns it could "return fire" against violations.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, GlobalSecurity, UNIFIL reporting confirmed.

text: Day 39 summary: DEADLINE DAY. Oil volatile — Brent $110.05 (+37.8%), WTI $113.42 (+50.2%). Gas ~$4.12. HRANA: 3,540+ killed in Iran. US: 13 KIA. Lebanon: 1,461 dead. Hegseth: most intense strikes ever today. No ceasefire. No diplomacy. 8pm ET tonight.
verificationNote: Aggregated from Al Jazeera, PBS, NPR, Bloomberg, AAA, Oil Price API.
